skill.name: clinical-decision-support
skill.description: Generate clinical decision support documents for pharmaceutical and clinical research teams. The skill creates cohort analyses, biomarker summaries, treatment recommendation reports, evidence grading, decision algorithms, and LaTeX-ready outputs.
skill.summary: Create research-focused clinical decision support reports with evidence grading and biomarker analysis.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found most were false positives from clinical language, Markdown examples, and LaTeX syntax. Real concerns are local file writes, user-controlled LaTeX generation, and handling of clinical datasets without enforced de-identification. No prompt injection, hidden network access, credential theft, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: User Input Is Written Into LaTeX Without Escaping
medium_findings.description: The decision tree helper inserts text and JSON fields into TikZ and LaTeX strings. Malformed or hostile input could alter generated documents before compilation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly interpolates user-supplied text into LaTeX output. I did not find shell execution, so the impact is document manipulation rather than confirmed code execution.

medium_findings.title: Unrestricted Local Output Path Writes
medium_findings.description: Helper scripts write generated files to caller-controlled paths and directories. This can overwrite local files if the operator supplies unsafe output locations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple scripts call open() or to_csv() with user-influenced output paths. This is expected tool behavior, but there is no path restriction or overwrite guard.

medium_findings.title: Clinical Data Is Read And Re-Emitted Locally
medium_findings.description: The scripts load patient cohort CSV files and create derived CSV, TXT, TEX, PDF, and PNG outputs. The skill advises de-identification, but scripts do not enforce it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows local ingestion and export of clinical datasets. The risk depends on operator data handling, so this is a privacy and governance concern.

low_findings.title: Bash-Enabled Workflow Requires Operator Review
low_findings.description: The skill allows Bash and documents script execution for figures and dependencies. I found command examples, but no hidden subprocess calls or credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow intentionally uses Bash-capable tooling. Static external-command hits were mostly Markdown examples, but users should review commands before running them.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts are fenced examples or inline script names in documentation. They are not executed by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown code fences or examples. I did not find Python subprocess, os.system, or network command execution in the reviewed scripts.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Clinical Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in clinical descriptions, template placeholders, or algorithm text. I found no cryptographic API use at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain clinical decision support prose and LaTeX templates. The apparent matches are substring collisions, not MD5, SHA1, DES, or related code.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Medical Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: System and network reconnaissance alerts map to clinical terminology such as biomarkers, trial search terms, and immunotherapy abbreviations. No recon behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content discusses biomarkers and treatment guidance. I found no host enumeration, port scanning, network requests, or environment probing.

dangerous_patterns.title: User Input Is Written Into LaTeX Without Escaping
dangerous_patterns.description: The decision tree helper inserts text and JSON fields into TikZ and LaTeX strings. Malformed or hostile input could alter generated documents before compilation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code directly interpolates user-supplied text into LaTeX output. I did not find shell execution, so the impact is document manipulation rather than confirmed code execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unrestricted Local Output Path Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: Helper scripts write generated files to caller-controlled paths and directories. This can overwrite local files if the operator supplies unsafe output locations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Multiple scripts call open() or to_csv() with user-influenced output paths. This is expected tool behavior, but there is no path restriction or overwrite guard.

dangerous_patterns.title: Clinical Data Is Read And Re-Emitted Locally
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts load patient cohort CSV files and create derived CSV, TXT, TEX, PDF, and PNG outputs. The skill advises de-identification, but scripts do not enforce it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows local ingestion and export of clinical datasets. The risk depends on operator data handling, so this is a privacy and governance concern.

content.value_statement: Clinical research teams need structured reports that combine evidence, biomarkers, statistics, and treatment logic. This skill generates research-focused CDS documents with GRADE grading, cohort analysis, and publication-ready LaTeX outputs.
actual_capabilities: Creates patient cohort analysis documents with biomarker stratification and outcome comparisons.
Generates treatment recommendation reports using GRADE-style evidence and recommendation strength.
Builds clinical decision algorithms and TikZ flowcharts for treatment pathways.
Produces survival analysis outputs, cohort tables, and biomarker classification summaries through helper scripts.
Uses LaTeX templates for publication-ready clinical research and pharmaceutical documents.
limitations: It is not a medical device and does not replace clinician judgment.
It requires de-identified data because scripts do not enforce HIPAA de-identification.
Generated recommendations need review against current guidelines and local standards.
LaTeX and Python dependencies must be available for full PDF and analysis workflows.

use_cases.title: Trial subgroup analysis
use_cases.description: Analyze response, PFS, OS, and safety by biomarker-defined groups in a study cohort.

use_cases.title: Evidence-based recommendation report
use_cases.description: Summarize treatment options, guideline evidence, GRADE strength, and decision logic for a disease area.

use_cases.title: Biomarker-guided pathway design
use_cases.description: Create treatment selection algorithms using genomic, expression, IHC, and clinical criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic cohort report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a patient cohort analysis for a de-identified oncology cohort. Stratify by biomarker group and summarize baseline features, response, PFS, OS, and safety.

prompt_templates.title: Draft treatment recommendations
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate evidence-based treatment recommendations for a disease state. Include evidence tables, GRADE strength, monitoring considerations, and a decision algorithm.

prompt_templates.title: Build a biomarker pathway
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a biomarker-guided treatment pathway using molecular status, performance status, prior therapy, and contraindications. Include decision points and escalation rules.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a regulatory research package
prompt_templates.prompt: Produce a pharmaceutical research CDS package with executive summary, cohort tables, survival analysis, biomarker interpretation, evidence grading, and review limitations.

output_examples.input: A researcher provides de-identified NSCLC outcomes stratified by PD-L1 expression.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a cohort report with group characteristics, response rates, PFS and OS summaries, biomarker interpretation, and clinical implications.

output_examples.input: A medical affairs team asks for HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer recommendations.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a recommendation report with evidence tables, GRADE-labeled options, monitoring guidance, and a treatment sequencing pathway.

output_examples.input: A strategy team needs a biomarker decision algorithm for trial planning.
output_examples.output: The skill drafts a pathway with molecular eligibility criteria, treatment branches, evidence notes, and limitations for expert review.

best_practices: Use only de-identified cohort data and remove direct patient identifiers before analysis.
Verify all recommendations against current guidelines, labels, and local clinical policy.
Review generated LaTeX and script outputs before compiling or distributing documents.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for bedside decisions or emergency care instructions.
Do not paste identifiable patient data into prompts, CSV files, or templates.
Do not publish generated recommendations without clinical, statistical, and compliance review.

faq.question: Can this skill make patient-specific treatment decisions?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for research and group-level evidence documents, not bedside patient management.

faq.question: Does it support GRADE methodology?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates and references include recommendation strength and evidence quality grading.

faq.question: Can it analyze biomarker-stratified cohorts?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports PD-L1, HER2, genomic alterations, molecular subtypes, and custom biomarker thresholds.

faq.question: Does it create publication-ready outputs?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes LaTeX templates and scripts for tables, survival curves, and clinical pathways.

faq.question: Is patient privacy enforced automatically?
faq.answer: No. Users must de-identify inputs and review all generated files for protected health information.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The skill is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
